extra.reasoning: The property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) holds across the entire input space. rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, which requires all four of {hepatic_impairment, seizure_history, g6pd_deficiency, ckd_dialysis} = true; otherwise the default leaves it false. In exactly that four-condition case, both rules affecting rec_drug_b match: r4 (priority 90) sets rec_drug_b := false, and r5 (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true. Per-output priority resolution picks the highest-priority matching rule, so r4 (90) beats r5 (50) and rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b is forced to false by r4, and the two can never be jointly true. r4's elevated priority is precisely the guard that prevents this conflict. No counterexample exists.
